title: Internet in China for Tourists
metaDescription: Essential tips for tourists on using the internet, apps, and mobile data while traveling in China.

sections.heading: Getting Started
sections.content: 1. Prepare a VPN (Virtual Private Network) app before arrival to access international sites and apps. 2. Purchase a local SIM card or portable Wi-Fi at the airport for reliable mobile data. 3. Download essential Chinese apps like WeChat, Alipay, and a translation app beforehand.

sections.heading: Key Tips
sections.content: A. WeChat is essential for messaging, payments, and some services—set it up with your international card. B. Use offline maps and translation features in apps like Baidu Maps or Google Translate (via VPN). C. Many public places offer free Wi-Fi, but a local data plan is more reliable for navigation and communication.

sections.heading: What to Remember
sections.content: 1. Mobile payments via WeChat Pay or Alipay are ubiquitous; cash is less commonly used. 2. Some apps and websites are blocked; your VPN must be active to use services like Gmail, WhatsApp, or Instagram. 3. Always have key addresses and destinations written in Chinese characters to show taxi drivers or locals.

keyTakeaways: Prepare a VPN and essential apps before your trip.
Use WeChat and local mobile data for seamless payments and communication.
Keep important information in Chinese for easy navigation.
